title: Risham Nominees Pty Ltd (trading as Centenary Bakehouse) — Public safety
investigation_objective: Risham Nominees Pty Ltd, trading as Centenary Bakehouse, was convicted of workplace manslaughter - only the second such conviction under Victoria's workplace manslaughter provisions since their 2020 introduction - and fined $3.4 million, a record penalty, over the death of worker Jassim Al Shadori, who fell approximately 4.5 metres through a roof space while removing insulation panels at the company's Reservoir bakery on 1 August 2021. WorkSafe Victoria found no fall-prevention controls had been applied before the roof works began, despite harnesses being available but not required to be used.
scope: Convicted of workplace manslaughter, only the second such conviction in Victoria since the offence was introduced in 2020; Fined $3.4 million, a record penalty for the offence; WorkSafe found no fall-prevention controls were required or applied despite equipment being available
